description.task: Simulate the behavior of JUnit 5's core annotations (@Test, @BeforeEach, @AfterEach, @BeforeAll, @AfterAll) by implementing a custom test runner that respects lifecycle hooks.
instructions: In run(), find all methods sorted by annotation type.
Invoke @BeforeAll methods first (static — invoke on null or with testClass).
For each @Test method: invoke @BeforeEach method(s), then the @Test method, then @AfterEach method(s).
description.hint: JUnit 5's test lifecycle is crucial for test isolation. @BeforeEach creates fresh state before each test so one test's side effects don't affect another.
description.expectedOutput:
[BeforeAll] Setting up test suite
[BeforeEach] Preparing test context
[Test] testReverse: PASS
[AfterEach] Cleaning up after test
[BeforeEach] Preparing test context
[Test] testUpperCase: PASS
[AfterEach] Cleaning up after test
[AfterAll] Test suite complete

description.task: Implement a parameterized test runner that runs the same test method with multiple input sets. Demonstrate testing a method with various inputs and expected outputs.
instructions: Run Main as-is — the framework and test cases are already set up.
Verify all email and phone validation tests pass.
If any test fails, fix the StringValidator methods accordingly.
The output should show all PASSes.
description.hint: JUnit 5 @ParameterizedTest with @MethodSource, @CsvSource, or @ValueSource runs the same test with different inputs. This reduces duplicate test code significantly.
description.expectedOutput:
Test: isValidEmail
  PASS: input='user@example.com'              expected=true actual=true
  PASS: input='invalid-email'                  expected=false actual=false
  PASS: input='@example.com'                   expected=false actual=false
  PASS: input=''                               expected=false actual=false
  PASS: input='a@b.org'                        expected=true actual=true
Test: isValidPhone
  PASS: input='1234567890'                     expected=true actual=true
  PASS: input='123'                            expected=false actual=false
  PASS: input='12345678901'                    expected=false actual=false
  PASS: input='abcdefghij'                     expected=false actual=false

Results: 9 passed, 0 failed

description.task: Write a comprehensive test suite for an OrderService using custom assertion methods that produce readable failure messages. Test creation, calculation, and state transitions.
description.context: JUnit 5's assertAll() runs multiple assertions and reports all failures, not just the first. Custom assertion methods improve test readability and maintainability.
instructions: testOrderCreation(): assert order.getId()==1, items.size()==2, total==1029.98 (delta 0.01), status==PENDING.
testOrderConfirmation(): assert confirm() returns false on empty order; add item, assert confirm() returns true; assert status==CONFIRMED; assert second confirm() returns false.
testOrderCancellation(): assert cancel() returns false when SHIPPED; assert status remains SHIPPED.
Run Main as-is after implementing the TODO sections.
description.hint: JUnit 5's assertAll() is great for grouping related assertions. Use assertThrows() for exception testing and assertThat() with Hamcrest matchers for fluent assertions.
description.expectedOutput:
Test: Order Creation
  PASS: order id
  PASS: item count
  PASS: total price
  PASS: initial status
Test: Order Confirmation
  PASS: cannot confirm empty order
  PASS: confirm with items
  PASS: status after confirm
  PASS: cannot confirm twice
Test: Order Cancellation
  PASS: cannot cancel shipped order
  PASS: status remains shipped

Total: 10 assertions, 0 failed
